key_insight: Separating physicochemical descriptors and molecular graph structure into two additive branches makes aqueous solubility predictions more interpretable, showing what comes from chemistry versus topology.
clinical_relevance: Better solubility prediction can help early drug discovery by flagging compounds with formulation or developability risks sooner, before costly downstream testing.
method: Chemical branch: multilayer perceptron on physicochemical descriptors
Structural branch: graph neural network on molecular graphs
Additive fusion at prediction time, with optional multiplicative interaction
evidence_signal: The model was pretrained on the larger AqSolDB dataset and fine-tuned on the smaller BigSolDB2 dataset, which improved accuracy and reduced run-to-run variation while remaining competitive in both datasets.
watch_item: Interpretability findings depend on post hoc projections and GNNExplainer masks, so the chemical-versus-structural split should be treated as informative rather than definitive causality.
paper_title: An Additive MLP-GNN Framework for Characterizing Chemical and Structural Contributions to Aqueous Solubility
